content: Hey @Kanchan Borole This is the channel we will use to track your GSoC progress and discussions
On that note, Welcome to the chat!
Thank you @Divyanshu Garg. Looking forward to getting started!

Awesome, since we are in the community bonding period, we can take it as an opportunity to get more familiar with what we are working with.
Here are our goals for the next 20 days:-

  1. Project setup (Dev environment).
  2. Going through the repository, documentation, and other resources.
  3. Scope building - This is as important as writing down the actual code; remember, managing the project plays a huge role in the project's success and completion.
  • What we need is a well-defined and organized board that lists out the tasks/features we are going to be working on. This will let us know that we are on track. You can use GitHub issues/projects for that (In case you want to use any other tool, feel free to do that)
  • Every task and feature will consist of a description/explanation of what exactly it covers.
  • You send PRs out based on those issues and tasks.
  • This clears out the expectations and keeps clarity on what exactly we are working on and what's done.
  • The point is, good documentation is really important, so we have to maintain it that way.

Once this is all done, we will potentially be ready for the coding period.

Another important aspect of contribution would be communication,

  • Be clear about the availability; it doesn't have to be like a 24/7 task, but as a simple example, if you are gonna be away for a day, week, or any period of time due to exams, travel, or any other potential reason, inform them beforehand.
  • This creates a sense of clarity and trust, since in opposite scenarios, it creates problems for the ORG to calculate the progress and continuation of the project. Example - Disappearing for 15 days without notice might give mentors the impression that the contributor abandoned the GSoC program mid-way. It might sound absurd, but trust me, we have seen this happening a lot in recent years. (In terms of recent generation its called ghosting)
  • Never hesitate to ask questions, remember GSoC efforts are focused on a few agendas, one being to help new contributors learn.
  • Sharing Updates - can play a very good role in keeping the conversation active and letting the ORG and mentors know what's happening around the project.
  • You can choose to put casual updates regularly or choose an SOD or EOD plan to give yourself daily goals and see if you were able to achieve those goals.

With that, I am Divyanshu, and we will be working together for the next few months to deal with the problem statements proposed. Excited about the good things we are gonna be achieving by the end of it.

Thank you for the detailed overview! I will keep all of this in mind. I will start with setting up the development environment and going through the repository and documentation over the next few days. I will keep you updated on my progress regularly.
